Saponaria officinalis, also known as soapwort, is a perennial plant in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) that is native to Europe. It has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Saponaria officinalis include saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Saponaria officinalis is often used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and skincare products, due to its emollient and cleansing properties.
